intent: Post-workout meal — need high protein, generous portions, and somewhere I can eat fast without feeling rushed

mockPlace.name: Raku
mockPlace.address: 68 Carlton St, Toronto, ON M5B 1L6, Canada

mockReviews.author: Alex N
mockReviews.text: Best ramen in Toronto hands down. The tonkotsu broth is rich and flavorful. Huge portions — I could barely finish. The chashu pork is melt-in-your-mouth. Fast service too, food came out in under 10 minutes.

mockReviews.author: Emily C
mockReviews.text: Great ramen spot with authentic Japanese flavors. The miso ramen was excellent. Love that you can customize your noodle firmness and broth richness. Casual atmosphere, good for a quick lunch.

mockReviews.author: Ryan J
mockReviews.text: Solid ramen. The extra noodles and extra chashu are worth the add-on. This is not a dainty bowl — you leave full. Service is efficient, they don't rush you but the pace is naturally quick.

mockReviews.author: Lisa M
mockReviews.text: Good but not amazing. The broth was a bit too salty for my taste. The gyoza were the highlight. Small and cramped seating, definitely not a place to linger. Portions are decent though.

mockReviews.author: Kevin H
mockReviews.text: My go-to spot after gym sessions. The protein-heavy options like extra chashu and ajitama egg keep me coming back. Quick in and out, exactly what I need after a workout.

humanRationale: Post-workout intent implies portion size, food quality (protein focus), and speed. The LLM should infer these from context even though 'post-workout' and 'protein' are not in the keyword list.
